content.problemStatement: You are given two binary strings AAA and BBB, each with length NNN. You may reorder the characters of AAA in an arbitrary way and reorder the characters of BBB also in an arbitrary (not necessarily the same) way. Then, you should compute the XOR of the resulting strings. Find the number of distinct values of this XOR which can be obtained, modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007 (109+710^9 + 7109+7).
constraints: AAA and BBB contain only characters '0' and '1'
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 101010
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1032 \cdot 10^32‚čÖ103
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains a single string AAA with length NNN.
The third line contains a single string BBB with length NNN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the number of unique XORs modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: The characters in each string can be reordered in two ways (swap them or do nothing), so there are four values of their XOR:

"00" XOR "10" is "10"
"00" XOR "01" is "01"
"00" XOR "10" is "10"
"00" XOR "01" is "01"

There are only two distinct values.
